user_query:
We’re a mid-sized consumer electronics manufacturer (EuroElectro Devices GmbH) with EU and US supply chains. Please read the files under input/ and produce the following deliverables under output/ exactly as specified.

Inputs to read:
- input/topics.csv — list of ESG topics we want assessed for double materiality.
- input/emissions.csv — our base-year (2025) GHG activity totals by scope/subcategory.
- input/company_profile.json — basic profile incl. revenue_million for intensity.

Deliverables to create:
1) Double materiality matrix at output/materiality_matrix.csv
   - Columns (exactly): Topic, ImpactScore, FinancialScore, PrioritySegment, StakeholderGroups, ESRSReference
   - Use integer scores 1–5 for ImpactScore and FinancialScore.
   - Allowed StakeholderGroups values only: Employees;Customers;Suppliers;Communities;Shareholders;Environment;Regulators (semicolon-separated, at least one per topic).
   - PrioritySegment MUST follow this rule for every row:
     • If ImpactScore >= 4 AND FinancialScore >= 4 → "High"
     • Else if exactly one of ImpactScore or FinancialScore is >= 4 → "Priority"
     • Else if ImpactScore <= 2 AND FinancialScore <= 2 → "Monitor"
     • Else → "Consider"
   - Ensure the Topic column includes, at minimum, these topics (you may include more): GHG Emissions; Energy; Water; Waste; Human Rights; DEI; Data Privacy; Anti-Corruption; Supply Chain Due Diligence; Product Safety; Climate Risk; Biodiversity.
   - ESRSReference should start with "ESRS" and cite a relevant topical code (e.g., "ESRS E1-1", "ESRS S1-1", "ESRS G1-1").

2) GHG inventory summary at output/ghg_inventory.json
   - JSON object with keys: company, base_year, unit, revenue_million, totals, intensity_per_revenue, detail.
   - Set company = "EuroElectro Devices GmbH", base_year = 2025, unit = "tCO2e".
   - totals must include: scope1, scope2_location_based, scope2_market_based, scope3, total_ghg.
   - detail must be an array summing to totals, with at least:
     • Two or more Scope 1 subcategories (e.g., stationary combustion, mobile combustion)
     • Scope 2 entries for both location-based and market-based
     • Two or more Scope 3 category entries (e.g., Category 1 Purchased goods and services; Category 11 Use of sold products)
   - Compute intensity_per_revenue = total_ghg / revenue_million (from input/company_profile.json). Ensure total_ghg equals the sum of all detail tCO2e entries.

3) Implementation roadmap at output/esg_roadmap.md
   - Structure the roadmap using these exact phase headings:
     • Foundation (Months 1–3)
     • Strategy & Policy (Months 3–6)
     • Integration (Months 6–12)
     • Reporting & Improvement (Month 12+)
   - Tailor to consumer electronics; include double materiality, supplier audits, governance (board oversight, exec pay linkage), SBTi commitment, and independent assurance.

4) Supplier due diligence plan at output/supplier_due_diligence.md
   - Reference and organize the plan around the OECD Due Diligence Framework’s six steps: Embed; Identify & Assess; Cease, Prevent, Mitigate; Track; Communicate; Remediate.
   - Explicitly address legal regimes relevant to us: UFLPA, EU Deforestation Regulation, and CSDDD.
   - Include risk scoring, grievance mechanism, and remediation approach.

5) Communications guardrails at output/communications_guardrails.md
   - A concise note with anti-greenwashing rules (make claims substantiated, use precise language, obtain third-party verification, disclose challenges and missed targets).

Please keep all outputs in English, avoid vague claims, and align guidance with professional best practices. Do not write to any paths outside output/.
